titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Sir Joseph Banks, Correspondence
titlestmt.author: by Beinecke Staff

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

controlnote.p: This finding aid, which is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0., has a MARC record in Yale's ILS with the following bib number: 7042011.

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

conventiondeclaration.abbr: beinecke manuscript unit archival processing manual
conventiondeclaration.citation: translation missing: en.enumerations.resource_finding_aid_description_rules.beinecke manuscript unit archival processing manual

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Friday May 7, 2021 at 03:38

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract:
Autograph MS.
Collection of autograph drafts of letters to various recipients, including a few letter to Banks from various correspondents, concerning scientific matters, the Royal Society, etc.; includes portrait engraving.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: For provenance information, consult the appropriate curator.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Osborn fd10 is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Sir Joseph Banks, Correspondence. James Marshall and Marie-Louise Osborn Collection, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: This finding aid was produced from a previously existing list or card set. All pertinent bibliographical information has been retained; the volume may contain additional unlisted material.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The following finding aid is a partial inventory of the contents of Osborn fd10. This list was reformatted from previously existing cataloging.

persname.part: Delambre, J. B. J. (Jean Baptiste Joseph), 1749-1822

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p:
Office for Trade [London]
1 p.; 23 x 18 cm.
Request for Banks to peruse an
enclosure [enclosure missing];
endorsed by Banks.
"Banks Letters," p. 12.
fd.10/12

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p:
Soho Square, London
1 p.; 23 x 18 cm.
Concerns agriculture and the Duke of
Bedford.
"Banks Letters," p. 20.
fd. 10/4

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p:
[Soho Square, London]
1 p.; 23 x 19 cm.
Concerns the Royal Society of London.
"Banks Letters," p. 26.
fd.10/26

did.unittitle: AL in 3d person to Henry Bone (1755-1834)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p:
Soho Square, London
1 p.; 19 x 11 cm
Letter of regrets declining an
invitation.
"Banks Letters," p.120.
fd.10/1

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p:
[Soho Square, London]
1 p.; 23 x 18 cm.
Concerns a portrait lent to Banks by
Briquet.
"Banks Letters," p. 153.
fd.10/25

did.unittitle: AL (draft) to [Antoine Alexis Francois Cadet de Vaux (1743-1828)]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p:
Soho Square, London
1 p.; 23 x 19 cm.
Concerns a discovery by Cadet de
Vaux.
"Banks Letters," p. 191.
fd.10/6

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p:
Overton
2 p.; 24 x 19 cm.
Concerns Chenevix's researches in
chemistry.
"Banks Letters," p. 215.
fd.10/22

did.unittitle: ALS (draft) to Jean Baptiste Joseph Delambre (1749-1822)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p:
Soho Square, London
4 p.; 20 x 16 cm.
Concerns English members of the Royal
Society who are held as prisoners in
France; expresses gratitude for the
release of a Mr. Osborn and seeks
release of Mr. Robert Ferguson.
"Banks Letters," p. 256.
fd.10/10

did.unittitle: AL (draft) to Jean Baptiste Joseph Delambre (1749-1822)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p:
Soho Square, London
1 p.; 25 x 20 cm.
Concerns nautical almanacs to be
published.
"Banks Letters," p. 256.
fd.10/18

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p:
[Soho Square, London]
2 p.; 23 x 19 cm.
Concerns Banks's application to the
National Institute of France for
Ferguson's release from prison.
"Banks Letters," p. 325.
fd.10/19

did.unittitle: AL (draft) to John Foster, later Lord Oriel, (1740-1828)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p:
Soho Square, London
1 p.; 20 x 16 cm.
Concerns a proposed statue in memory
of a duke.
"Banks Letters," p. 341.
fd.10/8

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p:
[Revesby Abbey, Lincolnshire]
1 p.; 23 x 18 cm.
Concerns sheep; also includes 2
drawings of the hind-quarters of
sheep.
"Banks Letters," p. 373.
fd.10/29

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p:
[Soho Square]
1 p.; 23 x 18 cm.
Expresses thanks for books sent to
Banks; Banks also states that he
has delivered books to the Count de
[Bournans] and Mr. Greville.
"Banks Letters," p. 402.
fd.10/28

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p:
Soho Square
2 p.; 24 x 19 cm.
Concerns proposed additions to the
collections of the British Museum
relating to animal anatomy.
"Banks Letters," p. 408.
fd.10/2

did.unittitle: AL (draft) to Sir Everard Home, 1st bart. (1756-1832)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p:
Spring Grove
2 p.; 23 x 18 cm.
Cites evidence that birds have
reason.
"Banks Letters," p. 419.
fd.10/15

did.unittitle: AL (draft) to Charles Jenkinson, 1st earl of Liverpool (1727-1808)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p:
Soho Square, London
2 p.;23 x 18 cm.
Discusses a Mr. Pit [?William Pitt
(1759-1806)] and the appointment of
a deputy.
"Banks Letters," p. 467.
fd.10/20

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p:
Soho Square, London
1 p.; 22 x 18 cm.
Writing on behalf of the African
Association to the British Consul in
Tangiers with regards to an
expedition to explore Africa.
"Banks Letters".
fd.10/5

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p:
Soho Square, London
2 p.;32 x 20 cm.
Banks declines a requests that he
serve as an arbitrator.
"Banks Letters," p. 690.
fd.10/3

descriptivenote.p: [Letter from] Sir Joseph Bansk [to] Bartholomew Rudd August 16, 1801. [24th letter.]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p:
Soho Square, London
1 p.;23 x 18 cm.
Agrees to plead the cause of the
sister of Captain James Cook
(1728-1779) and discusses Capt.
Cook's widow.
"Banks Letters," p. 722.
fd.10/24

did.unittitle: AL (draft) to Sir Benjamin Thompson, Count Rumford (1753-1814)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p:
[Soho Square, London]
2 p.; 23 x 19 cm.
Discusses the affairs of the Royal
Society as well as his personal
health.
"Banks Letters," p. 816.
fd.10/23

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p:
Soho Square
2 p.; 20 x 16 cm.
Concerns rolls.
"Banks Letters," p. 835.
fd.10/21

did.unittitle: ALS (draft) to Charles Grant, vicomte de Vaux (b. 1749)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p:
Soho Square
2 p.; 21 x 16 cm.
Concerns essays and writings relating
to the Royal Society.
"Banks Letters," p. 366.
fd.10/9

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p:
Soho Square, London
2 p.; 11 x 18 cm.
Concerns the Royal Society and
introduces a Mr. Bickersteth [?
Henry Bickersteth, Baron Langdale
(1783-1851)] and thanks for being
introduced to a Dr. [? Monk].
"Banks Letters," p. 847.
fd.10/7

did.unittitle: AL (draft) to the Directors of the West India Dock Company

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p:
[Soho Square, London]
1 p.; 22 x 18 cm.
Concerns Lord Seeforth, Governor of
Barbados, and bringing plants from
Cayenne to the Royal Gardens at Kew.
"Banks Letters," p. 865.
fd.10/17

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p:
[Soho Square, London]
2 p.; 21 x 16 cm.
Concerns the concerns experiments in
agriculture and agricultural
chemistry.
"Banks Letters," p. 889.
fd.10/16

did.unittitle: ALS to Sir Joseph Banks, bart. (1743-1820)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p:
Crawley, Sussex
1 p.; 32 x 20 cm.
Concerns the National Plan and
mentions Lord Sheffield.
"Banks Letters," p. 239.
fd.10/11

did.unittitle: AL in 3rd person to Sir Joseph Banks (1743-1820)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p:
B: House
1 p.; 23 x 19 cm.
Informs Banks that the nymphaea is
now in flower.
"Banks Letters," p. 45.
Women Writers
fd.10/14
